notes: pi 0.70.0 rewinds IN PLACE: `/tree` opens an interactive tree-selector overlay; selecting an earlier entry calls session.navigateTree → SessionManager.branch(), which only moves the in-memory leaf pointer ('existing entries are not modified or deleted') and keeps the SAME append-only .jsonl session file under the SAME session_id. The next turn appends a child of the rewound entry to that same file — an in-place rewind, like claudecode (NOT a fork like codex). LAZY-MATERIALIZATION SCOPE NOTE: contrary to earlier docs that called pi 'non-lazy', the live recording proved pi creates the per-session JSONL file only AFTER the first user message commits — the file appears with turn 1's user+assistant lines already batched in, so the daemon's transcript_new fires after the file already contains turn 1 and NO `working→ready` state_transition is emitted for turn 1's arc. (Same shape as the multi-turn-conversation recording, where the spec also collapses turn 1 into session_birth + a synthetic instant arc.) The recipe therefore sends THREE prompts (one/two/three) but only TWO turn arcs are daemon-observable — `pre_rewind_turn` here is the recipe's `two` turn (the most recent pre-rewind observable checkpoint; the user's tree picker walks back to the `assistant: one` leaf), and `post_rewind_turn` is the post-rewind `three` turn. The rewind itself remains unobservable (branch() writes nothing at selection time); `post_rewind_ready` asserts a bounded dwell where state stays ready (no working transition before turn 3 starts) — same shape as the claudecode/checkpoint-rewind spec, which is also unable to observe the rewind event itself. WHY same_session_as pins: pi launches a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready that bridges until the UUID rollout transcript appears (same as the pi/multi-turn cell); the turn arcs all pin to the rollout session, so pre_rewind_turn anchors to recording start unpinned (first working ⇒ rollout) and every later phase pins via same_session_as. HOW runs differ: assistant wording, per-turn tokens, sub-second timing (model variance). Stable structurally: two ready→working→ready arcs on ONE append-only session_id, no new session ever created, a single process_exited, final state ready, post-rewind user prompt parented to the assistant-`one` entry in the transcript tree (NOT to assistant-`two`).

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch

First observable working arc — the recipe's `two` turn (turn 1's user/assistant pair is batched into the file before transcript_new fires, so the daemon emits no working transition for it). Binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working

`two` turn reply completes → back to ready; this is the most recent observable checkpoint before the user opens /tree (the conversation tree contains turns 1 and 2; the user will rewind to the `assistant: one` leaf via Up Up + Enter + Enter)

After /tree → Up Up → Enter → Enter (dismissing the `Summarize branch?` dialog with `No summary`), the session stays in ready for at least 3s: the rewind itself is not a turn, branch() writes nothing at selection time, and no transcript_removed / transcript_new fires (same observability shape as claudecode/checkpoint-rewind's post_rewind_ready)

Post-rewind prompt (`three`), issued from the rewound point → working again on the SAME rollout session_id (in-place rewind, not a fork). In the transcript tree, this user message is parented to the assistant `one` entry, NOT to assistant `two`
Post-rewind reply completes → back to ready; turn arc is clean from the rewound checkpoint
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the post-rewind turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
